T he Facebook parent company was found to have contributed to a woman's social media addiction

items.title: Meta and YouTube found liable for social media addiction
items.standfirst: A jury in the US has found Meta and YouTube liable for social media addiction.
items.content: A jury in the US has found Meta and YouTube liable for social media addiction.

items.title: Meta struck down in child negligence decision
items.standfirst: T he Facebook parent company was found to have contributed to a woman's social media addiction.
items.content: A landmark court decision has found Meta and YouTube liable for child harm, forcing the owner of Facebook and Instagram into a $US3 million ($4.31 million) payout.A jury in California found the companies were "negligent in the design or operation of their platforms".A woman aged 20 brought the case against the tech giant, saying her use of social media as a child addicted her to the technology and "exacerbated her mental health struggles".LIVE UPDATES: Iran rejects US peace plan in blow to Donald Trump- Reported with Associated PressMore to come

items.title: Meta and Google found liable in landmark social media addiction trial
items.standfirst: A jury finds the tech giants were negligent in the design of their apps and failed to warn about their dangers.
items.content: A jury finds the tech giants were negligent in the design of their apps and failed to warn about their dangers.

items.title: 'Accountability has arrived': Meta and Google found liable in landmark social media addiction lawsuit
items.standfirst: The case was brought on by a 20-year-old woman who accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms which worsened her mental health.
items.content: The case was brought on by a 20-year-old woman who accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms which worsened her mental health.

clusters.title: Alleged ringleader of botched Baghsarian kidnapping to face court
clusters.neutral_headline: Alleged ringleader of botched Baghsarian kidnapping to face court
bullet_summary: Reported by The Age, 9News, ABC News Australia

items.title: Alleged ringleader of botched Baghsarian kidnapping to face court
items.standfirst: The alleged ringleader of the botched Chris Baghsarian abduction and murder will face a Sydney court today.
items.content: The alleged ringleader of the botched Chris Baghsarian abduction and murder will face a Sydney court today.

items.title: Identity of 'major player' charged with Sydney grandfather's murder revealed
items.standfirst: P olice allege the man played a role in facilitating the mistaken identity kidnapping and murder of a Sydney widower.
items.content: A 23-year-old man has been charged over the alleged kidnapping and murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian.Police will allege Delkin Donnelly, who was arrested at his Seven Hills home this morning, facilitated the kidnapping and recruited other people to carry out the detention of the 85-year-old Baghsarian.The widower was taken from his home in North Ryde on February 13th, though police later found he was not the intended target.LIVE UPDATES: Trump says 'the war is won'; Airstrikes batter IranHis remains were found 11 days later near a golf club in Pitt Town in Sydney's north-west. Detective Acting Superintendent Andrew Marks said police would also allege the 23-year-old was responsible for organising the disposal of Baghsarian's remains after he died at a property in Dural, where it is alleged he was detained."[We believe] he is not the highest, but a major player in this matter," Marks said."That he arranged the kidnapping, he facilitated others to assist in that kidnapping, he facilitated others to detain Mr Baghsarian, hold him at the property in Dural, and also gave directions in the disposal of the body," he alleged.Donnelly was charged with multiple offences, including murder, taking and detaining in company with intent to ransom, occasioning actual bodily harm, and possessing an unauthorised firearm.He was also hit with other weapons and drug charges after a shotgun, handgun and drugs were seized from his home, and was refused bail to appear in court tomorrow.Marks said a breakthrough came when CCTV of three people in the Dural area was released last week, as well as images of a blue Hyundai believed to be connected to the crime.READ MORE: $800m in suspicious trades before Trump announcement is treason, says Nobel-winning economistThe car had been found burnt out on February 26 at Glenbrook train station in the Blue Mountains. Marks said police will allege Donnelly was the man holding the phone with the torch on in the CCTV footage, and will also claim he was at Baghsarian's North Ryde home when he was taken.Donnely's partner was also arrested at the scene, but not in relation to the alleged kidnapping and murder of Baghsarian.She was charged with contravening a domestic violence order and was released on bail.The 23-year-old man is the third person arrested in connection with Baghsarian's death. Daniel Stevens, 24, and Gerard Andrews, 29, are alleged to have "participated in a joint criminal enterprise with other persons" that led to Baghsarian's death. The pair remain before the courts.READ MORE: Jetstar slashes flights to New Zealand as fuel costs bitePolice believe the man arrested today knew either Stevens or Andrews, but clarified that the pair were not pictured in the CCTV footage released last week. Marks said police believe up to nine people were involved in the alleged kidnapping and murder, and that they may have been part of different groups. He said the house at Dural, where Baghsarian was allegedly kept, would have acted as a meeting point for many of the alleged offenders.There was a group of people who played significant roles in the entire offence," he said.There was some crossover from the [North Ryde] house to Dural, and the disposal of remains."Those persons were all involved in certain aspects [and] the central point would have been Dural."NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
